title: Warm up
content: The slide presents three illustrated scenes of taxi drivers in different Chinese cities, each depicting a distinct driving experience and cultural/technological context. It introduces the theme of comparing cities through the perspective of taxi rides.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows heavy traffic with digital navigation and historic architecture in background
Chongqing scene shows a driver reading a book with mountainous, layered cityscape and cable car
Hangzhou scene shows a driver using a smartphone with AI/robot graphics and a lake/pagoda backdrop

title: An Evolving Nation: A Ride Through China
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme, using images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture from different cities. It sets up the metaphor of a 'ride through China' to explore urban development.
key_claims: Title: 'An Evolving Nation'
Subtitle: 'A Ride Through China'
Visuals include a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, Hongyadong (Chongqing), and a pagoda (likely Hangzhou)

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the first session's structure, using a city skyline graphic paired with a three-step process chain. It establishes a framework for investigating cities: identifying local features, development strategies, and resulting outcomes.
key_claims: Session 1 focuses on investigation and research
Process flow: Local Features → Development Strategies → Outcomes & Positive Impact

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This activity slide asks students to match three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) with their corresponding development strategies. Arrows indicate the correct pairings between cities and strategy labels.
key_claims: Beijing pairs with Technology & tradition integration (融合)
Chongqing pairs with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou pairs with Sustainable urban development
Note: the visual arrows suggest cross-matching, but likely intended pairing per common knowledge differs from arrow depiction

title: Session 2 Ask & Analyse: critical analysis & comparative insights
content: This slide details task instructions for the second session, dividing participants into two roles: city observers and city experts. Observers ask questions while experts answer using textual evidence.
key_claims: City observers select 1 question from a list to ask each group
City experts answer the question using text examples
Session 2 emphasizes critical analysis and comparative insights

title: Session 3 Promote Your City!
content: This slide describes a role-play activity where groups act as 'City Promotion Ambassadors' to create a 2-minute promotional speech for their city, incorporating either a fun story (taxi driver role) or strategic explanation (city experts role). Specific requirements include using key phrases and creating a slogan.
key_claims: Groups must create a 2-minute promotional speech
Taxi driver role shares a fun story about the city
City experts role explains development strategies
Speech must use at least 3 key phrases from the text
Speech must end with a creative slogan

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This concluding slide summarizes the three cities' driving scenes alongside their respective development strategy labels, reinforcing the connection between local features and tailored development approaches. It emphasizes the overarching theme of China as an evolving nation with city-specific strategies.
key_claims: Beijing scene linked to sustainable urban development
Chongqing scene linked to tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou scene linked to technology & tradition integration
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜)
China is described as 'an evolving nation' with unique development strategies per city
